item.title: Acoustic Chicago blues
item.description: Periodc. 1928–1950LocationChicagoInfluencesCountry bluesRagtimeHokumLinksRYM A guitar that swings instead of moans.

item.title: Electric Texas blues
item.description: Period1942–presentLocationDallas and Houston, later AustinInfluencesAcoustic Texas bluesJazzJump bluesLinksRYM A single-string line that phrases like a horn.

item.title: Swamp blues
item.description: Period1954–1970LocationBaton Rouge, recorded in Crowley, LouisianaInfluencesElectric bluesZydecoCajun musicLinksRYM The laziest beat in the blues: a shuffle that sounds half-asleep, drums brushed down to a whisper, tremolo guitar hanging in the humidity, and a singer who’d rather insinuate than sho...

item.title: Jump blues
item.description: PeriodLate 1930s–mid-1950sLocationKansas City, Los Angeles, New York, ChicagoInfluencesSwingBig bandBoogie woogieBluesLinksRYM A saxophone honks a two-bar riff, a piano hammers boogie woogie patterns underneath, and a singer half-shouts a lyric about drinking, dancing, or both at once over a rhythm...
